expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns in the codebase or skill instructions instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: Response states 'No live codebase was provided. The handoff plan is written against conventional Python CLI project structure (src-layout, Click or argparse, pyproject.toml). All file paths in the plan are placeholders that the implementing agent must verify.' References conventional structural patterns rather than inventing arbitrary design.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
expectations.evidence: Artifact written to outputs/.agents/scratchpad/cli-config-validation/handoff-plan.md with full absolute path provided in response's Artifact Status section. File exists on disk.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: Artifact contains ## Implementation Slices (4 slices), ## File-by-File Implementation Map (file-by-file bullet list), and ## Validation Plan with automated and manual steps.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.
expectations.evidence: Status: 'ready for implementation'; Track: 'Standard'; Recommended Next Step: 'implement directly' — all concrete, no bracket placeholders.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.
expectations.evidence: All entries in Relevant Findings include reasons: cli.py ('the CLI entry-point where the validator must be invoked; pattern assumed to use Click...'), config.py ('likely home for env-var names as constants'), tests/ ('existing test suite location; new test_config_validator.py must be added alongside'), pyproject.toml/setup.cfg ('no new runtime dependencies are needed').

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.
expectations.evidence: Three assumptions and one minor open question listed. One assumption carries explicit 'Risk if wrong (startup-gate assumption): If validation fires during --help, it may confuse users...' Risk framing is present; questions are bounded in scope.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: Artifact Status section shows 'Status: **ready for implementation**' and the full absolute path to the artifact.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: Recommended Design section states 'This is preferred over the main alternative (raising a custom exception and catching it in main()) because:' and lists three concrete reasons.

expectations.text: The Validation Plan section of the handoff artifact contains TDD completion, a code-simplifier refactor pass, and independent code-reviewer review as named discrete steps — not buried solely in the Implementation Slices section.
expectations.evidence: The ## Validation Plan section contains: pytest command for the new tests, run of existing test suite, two manual invocation checks, and a 'Review focus' about sys.exit and listing all vars. TDD completion, code-simplifier refactor pass, and code-reviewer review do not appear as named discrete steps in the Validation Plan.
